root: ^Norman: I remember when this town didn't even catch Tuna... not enough boats to go around. How'd you get this thing from the shore?
^Sam: Don't know, honestly, it must have been hanging out in a weird spot. I've never seen this before either.
^Norman: Before everyone was going crazy over Tuna, this town was tiny. Only thing worthwhile was our Rainbow Trout. Now every sushi restaurant in the world wants Tuna.
^Sam: Hey man, I won't complain about a nice paycheck today.
^Norman: Not gonna take it home? All my fishermen friends always ate the first big one they caught.
^Sam: Maybe I'll think about doing that.
